decision_title: crush-pkg: static-site capsules (bundle a site into a signed ECAP)
decision_reason: Enables 'publish a site as a portable capsule' without exosphere — the surfer sitecapsule investigation showed crush-pkg already has the ECAP format (EcapManifest + sections + Ed25519 sign) but was bytecode-only. Added a site module that bundles a directory of static web assets (each file a SHA-256 EcapSection) into a signed .ecap, plus extract (hash-verified round-trip → servable tree).
summary: Decision: crush-pkg: static-site capsules (bundle a site into a signed ECAP)

rejected_alternatives.reason: bincode (the ECAP wire format) cannot deserialize serde_json::Value — it needs deserialize_any, which non-self-describing formats lack. Used a reserved __site__.json section (plain bytes) instead, touching no shared struct.

outcome: New crates/crush-pkg/src/site.rs (build/write/extract_site_capsule) + CLI 'site' and 'site-extract' subcommands. 5 site tests + CLI smoke (signed build -> extract -> byte-identical). crush-pkg 44+8 tests green, workspace check green. decision_title: Published core crates (crush-errors, crush-cast, casm) v0.2.0 to crates.io
decision_reason: External dependents (openko/fabric, crush-symbols, mycelium-mobile, arniko) can now consume versioned registry deps instead of path deps. Preceded by a clean/format pass (rustfmt the never-formatted core crates, auto-safe clippy), metadata (keywords/categories/readme/homepage), and a licensing reconciliation.
summary: Decision: Published core crates (crush-errors, crush-cast, casm) v0.2.0 to crates.io

rejected_alternatives.option: triple-license OCPL/MIT/Apache:OCPL is not an SPDX identifier (crates.io rejects it) and 'at your option' nullifies its protocol-protection intent; OCPL belongs on the openko protocol layer, not foundation IR crates
OCPL-governed non-SPDX publish (license-file):unusual for a library, loses SPDX badge, contradicts the permissive intent for foundation crates

outcome: 3 crates live at 0.2.0; v0.2.0 tagged+pushed; license now clean dual MIT OR Apache-2.0; copyright 'Antarik / Exosphere Authors' -> 'The Crush Authors'; LICENSE-MIT+LICENSE-APACHE bundled per-crate; stale __pycache__ pyc untracked. Publish order crush-errors -> crush-cast/casm.

Cargo check --workspace fails in crush-net with rustls-related error. Investigated: rustls dep present in crush-net/Cargo.toml; src imports rustls symbols. Captured as dejavue threads.md ticket; full error verbatim preserved at /tmp/cargo-check-workspace.log. NOT FIXED in this turn (per scope discipline). Next-step ticket: feature-flag or version-pin rustls; OR pin at workspace level if transitive via reqwest/hyper; verify with cargo check --workspace.

Phase-1 fix attempt (BACKFILLED to timeline.jsonl on 2026-06-19 per fidelity-restoration request, see supersedes_workspace_rustls_resolved event following this one): dropped optional=true flag from the rustls dep line in crates/crush-net/Cargo.toml. Rationale at the time: since rustls is referenced in crates/crush-net/src/caps.rs and src/tcp.rs which compile unconditionally, dropping optional kept rustls unconditional so cargo check --workspace would see it linked. Result: cargo check --workspace FAILED with new error `feature tls includes dep:rustls, but rustls is not an optional dependency`. Phase-1 was insufficient on its own -- Cargos dep:<name> syntax in [features] entries REQUIRES the named dep to be marked optional=true. Claimed RESOLVED prematurely; corrected by the explicit supersedes_workspace_rustls_resolved event following this one, then by subsequent _v2 (drop both + feature entry), _v3 (textbook c-modified form), and _v3_final (verified landed). Originally referenced in chat captures and threads.md summaries but never appended to timeline.jsonl in the original session; this backfill restores chronological fidelity so future reader of timeline does not see a gap between the original investigation (workspace_rustls_investigated_captured ts 19:09:34) and the first actual fix-attempt resolution (workspace_rustls_resolved_v2 ts 22:42:00).

Explicit supersedes for workspace_rustls_resolved (the Phase-1 backfill one event earlier in this same timeline). The Phase-1 fix dropped optional=true from the rustls dep -- a half-measure that broke Cargo because the [features] block had tls=[dep:rustls, dep:webpki-roots] which requires both named deps to be optional. Phase-1 claimed RESOLVED on the assumption that dropping optional would let rustls link unconditionally, but Cargo enforced the dep: requirement independently. This supersedes event makes that correction explicit (per code-reviewer point 2 discipline: any RESOLVED claim must be followed by an explicit supersession marker rather than relying on chronologically-later entries to implicitly correct it). Captures what Phase-1 actually did (drop optional=true alone) and why it was insufficient. Actual fix path: supersedes_workspace_rustls_resolved_v2 (still addresses the _v2 premature claim) -> workspace_rustls_resolved_v3_final (the textbook c-modified form). The two supersedes events are not redundant: one tracks the Phase-1 premature; the other tracks the _v2 premature.

Phase-2 correction: option (c) incomplete -- dropping `optional = true` alone BROKE cargo check --workspace with `feature tls includes dep:rustls, but rustls is not an optional dependency`. Final fix (option c + dep: removal): removed `optional = true` from rustls dep AND removed the `tls = ["dep:rustls"]` feature entry from [features] section. Result: rustls unconditional (always-on, audio-name uniform with `tls` always-on in practice), feature section empty (dead dep:rustls entry replaced by deletion). Verification: cargo check --workspace GREEN; cargo check -p xtask GREEN; cargo test -p xtask 8 passed; 0 failed. Trade-off: downstream default-features = false can no longer opt out of TLS (theoretical since crush-ast private); revisit if ever published externally via re-introducing optional = true + default = ["tls"].

Explicit supersession of workspace_rustls_resolved_v2. _v2 dropped both optional=true from the rustls dep AND the tls feature entry from the [features] block in crush-net/Cargo.toml -- the resulting manifest achieved cargo check --workspace GREEN but sacrificed downstream opt-out (the fix abandoned the users API-level opt-out guarantee). Per code-reviewer point 2 discipline, any RESOLVED claim in this timeline must be followed by an explicit supersession marker rather than relying on chronologically-later entries to implicitly correct it. Pointer to actual fix path: workspace_rustls_resolved_v3_final captures the textbook (c)-modified form (rustls+webpki-roots both optional=true; [features] has tls + default = [tls]). Complementary marker to supersedes_workspace_rustls_resolved, which addresses the parallel Phase-1 premature-RESOLVED claim. Note: the rename supersedes_* -> *_superseded groups all supersession markers under the same event-name family so a future reader scanning by event prefix finds them in one cluster.

FINAL pivot to (c)-modified form after multi-iteration debug (Phase 1 partial, Phase 2 partial, Phase 3 successful after recovering from shell-escape corruption via heredoc-file python with os.replace atomic write). Result on disk: rustls dep optional=true+default-features=false; webpki-roots dep optional=true; [features] block had tls=[dep:rustls, dep:webpki-roots] AND default=[tls]. cargo check --workspace GREEN; cargo check -p xtask GREEN; cargo test -p xtask 8 passed; 0 failed (no regression). cargo tree -p crush-net confirms rustls+webpki-roots linked via default-on tls feature. Code-reviewer critical feedback: the (c)-modified form preserves API-level opt-out only; source-level opt-out is NOT in place -- crates/crush-net/src/{caps,tcp}.rs reference rustls/webpki-roots unconditionally (no #[cfg(feature = tls)] gating). cargo check -p crush-net --no-default-features returns E0433 (confirms ungated source). Followup tickets: (1) gate rustls/webpki-roots usage in src/*.rs behind #[cfg(feature = tls)] so downstream default-features = false actually compiles; (2) add cargo check --no-default-features to CI as both regression gate AND forcing-function for the source-gating fix; (3) bump rustls 0.23 -> 0.24+ and webpki-roots 0.26 -> 0.30+ when stable. threads.md resolution block rewritten to V3-FINAL reflecting both landed state + source-level followup flag.

Workspace-wide audit of `dep:<name>` references in [features] blocks across all crates. Scanned every Cargo.toml (excluding target/, .git/, node_modules/) via tomllib. Classifier: dep:<name> with optional=true = OK; dep:<name>? = SAFE-QUERY; dep:<name> without optional=true = AT-RISK (manifest error). decision_title: CRUSHTESTSSPLIT-1 regression: branch mismatch between script design and worktree source
decision_reason: The v2 atomic-split script was calibrated against agent/buffy/network (1179-line tests.rs with 18 banners, helpers at lines 0-153, arithmetic at line 154) but the CRUSHTESTSSPLIT-2 worktree was created from origin/main (660-line tests.rs with 16 banners, arithmetic at line 13). On origin/main, banners[0][0]=13 means helpers_lines = lines[:13] captured only imports. The matrix section is absent from origin/main entirely. Audit confirms origin/main uses identical naming convention; all 16 banners recognized by NAME_MAP. The multi-line banner theory was a misdiagnosis.

CRUSHTESTSSPLIT-1 (v3) landed — tests.rs split into 7 sub-files on agent/buffy/network via squash-merged PR #15. Next arcs should work from the split structure in tests/ rather than the monolithic tests.rs.
next: 1. Work from the split test structure under crates/crush-vm/src/tests/. 2. Add new tests under the appropriate sub-file. 3. Consider splitting portable_vm.rs tests using the same pattern.

decision_title: CRUSHTESTSSPLIT-1 (v3): atomic split of tests.rs into 7 sub-files landed on agent/buffy/network
decision_reason: 65 #[test] annotations split across 6 domain sub-files + mod.rs. v3 fixed two issues found in rounds 1-2: (1) branch mismatch — the v2 script was calibrated for agent/buffy/network's 1179-line tests.rs but ran against origin/main's 660-line version; (2) BANNER_RE indentation — ^// did not match the indented cross-parser matrix banner (fix: ^\s*//\s*). Added Gate 0 branch-mismatch pre-flight (abort if tests.rs < 700 lines). Verified: build green, 81 tests pass, per-fn diff IDENTICAL.
